title: quercus rubra var. pagodifolia
intro: Quercus rubra var. pagodifolia, also known as the Northern Red Oak, is a deciduous tree species native to eastern North America. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and bark, which contain tannins and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus rubra var. pagodifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support cardiovascular health. Traditionally, the herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures made from the leaves and bark, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
